name: xanthan gum
description: Xanthan gum is a natural polysaccharide thickener and stabilizer that controls viscosity and improves texture in food products.
raw_text: Xanthan Gum (Explained + Products) Products Ingredients Decode INCI Login Register Login Register Products Ingredients Decode INCI Follow us on our new Insta page » Xanthan Gum What-it-does: viscosity controlling , emulsion stabilising Official CosIng Information All Functions: binding, emulsifying, emulsion stabilising, gel forming, skin conditioning, surfactant, viscosity controlling Description: xanthan gum CAS #: 11138-66-2 | EC #: 234-394-2 Ph. Eur. Name: Gummi Xanthanum Details It's one of the most commonly used thickener s and emulsion stabilizers . If the product is too runny, a little xanthan gum will make it more gel-like. Used alone, it can make the formula sticky and it is a good team player so it is usually combined with other thickeners and so-called rheology modifiers (helper ingredients that adjust the flow and thus the feel of the formula). The typical use level of Xantha Gum is below 1%, it is usually in the 0.1-0.5% range. Btw, Xanthan gum is all natural, a chain of sugar molecules (polysaccharide) produced from individual sugar molecules ( glucose and sucrose ) via fermentation. It’s approved by Ecocert and also used in the food industry (E415). [more] [more] [less] Something incorrect or missing? Let us know!
